questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All studs are girly. Some girly are turks. Conclusions: I. Some studs are turks. II. Some girly are studs.

choices.b: Only I, II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All bricks are tricks. Some tricks are bricks. Conclusions: I. No brick is a brick. II. Some bricks are bricks. III. Some bicks are tricks. IV. Some tricks are not bricks.

choices.e: Either or II, and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No gardens are wardens. All gardens are locks. Conclusions: I. Some locks are wardens. II. Some locks are wardens. III. No lock is a warden. IV. Some locks are gardens.

choices.d: Either I or II and IV follow
choices.e: Either I or II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Only books are readable. Only eatables are books. Conclusions: I. All readables are books. II. Some readables are books. III. All readables are eatables. IV. All eatables are readables.

choices.c: Either I or II and III follow
choices.d: Either I or II and IV follow
